headline: Trump Says Weapons Makers Agree to Expand Production
summary: President Trump said America’s largest weapons makers have agreed to raise production of military munitions following “a very good meeting” at the White House on Friday.  Executives from BAE Systems, Boeing, Honeywell Aerospace, L3Harris Technologies, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman and RTX attended Friday’s meeting, Trump said.  “They have agreed to quadruple Production of the ‘Exquisite Class’ Weaponry in that we want to reach, as rapidly as possible, the highest levels of quantity,” Trump wrote in a social media post.

raw.headline: Trump Says Weapons Makers Agree to Expand Production

headline: U.S. Defense Manufacturers Face A Rare Earth Supply Squeeze - OilPrice.com Market Commentary
summary: According to sources, the Pentagon will prohibit the use of rare earth magnet materials originating from China in U.S. military platforms beginning in 2027. This mandate will have broad implications across the American defense industrial base, requiring manufacturers to verify the origin of the rare earth metals used in their systems, tracing them back to the earliest stages of the processing chain. Companies mentioned in this release include: REalloys Inc. (ALOY), Olin Corporation (NYSE: OLN),

headline: Had You Invested $1,000 in Northrop Grumman or Lockheed Martin a Decade Ago, Here’s What You’d Have Now
summary: On March 2, 2026, as news broke of U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear and military sites during Operation Epic Fury, defense stocks moved opposite to nearly everything else. Northrop Grumman (NYSE: NOC) jumped 4.6% in premarket trading, while Lockheed Martin (NYSE: LMT) rose more than 3%, even as the broader S&P 500 sold ... headline: 4 Winning Space Stocks To Buy Before The Artemis Launch
